LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANTS LIBERTY ASSET MANAGEMENT CORP., LUCY GAO, BENNY KO, RED DOT INVESTMENTS, INC & TED HSU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANTS LIBERTY ASSET MANAGEMENT CORP., LUCY GAO, AND BENNY KO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P.'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS, WITH PARTICULARITY, TO ESTABLISH A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. (CROSS-COMPLAINT, 13, 17-26.) CROSS-COMPLAINANT ALLEGES THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS, THROUGH THEIR AGENT, MISREPRESENTED THE EXISTING FACTS THAT THE POTENTIAL PURCHASERS WERE ALL CASH BUYERS WITH A UBS ACCOUNT CONTAINING ALL OF THE CASH TO BUY THE PROPERTY. (CROSS COMPLAINT, 13.) THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS OVERRULED AS CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS, WITH PARTICULARITY, THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS, THROUGH THEIR AGENT, NEGLIGENTLY MISREPRESENTED TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT THAT THE POTENTIAL PURCHASER WAS AN ALL CASH BUYER THAT HAD NO NEED FOR A PRE-CLOSING LOAN OR ADDITIONAL EQUITY. (CROSS-COMPLAINT, 28.) THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ALTER EGO IS OVERRULED AS CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT A UNITY OF INTEREST EXISTED BETWEEN CROSS-DEFENDANTS AND THAT ADHERENCE TO THE SEPARATE EXISTED WOULD SANCTION FRAUD AND PROMOTE INJUSTICE. (CROSS-COMPLAINT, 50-51.) THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE, IF IT CAN DO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD ADDITIONAL FACTS THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS HSU AND RED DOT INTENTIONALLY INDUCED CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TO RELY ON THEIR MISREPRESENTATION(S). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE:

Notice Of Motion And Oiton For Cost Recovery
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P. and HILL-UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Motion For Cost Recovery is denied, without prejudice. Defendant's motion is premature pending a final disposition of the issues raised in Defendants' unresolved cross-complaint. (Day v. Papadakis (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 503, 513, disapproved on another ground in Morehart v. County of Santa Barbara (1994) 7 Cal.4th 725.) "The language authorizing determination of who is the prevailing party "whether or not the suit proceeds to final judgment" (Civ. Code, 1717, subd. (b)(1)) does not imply that the suit need not be finally disposed of . . . ." (Bank of Idaho v. Pine Avenue Associates (1982) 137 Cal.App.3d 5, 15 disapproved on another ground in Snyder v. Marcus & Millichap (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1099.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF UN PLAZA LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One And Requests For Production, Set No. Two And Requests For Monetary Sanctions is Denied as applicable to discovery of Limited Partners identity, contribution or distributions, and any related categories of inquiry. The nature of a limited partner's relationship with an LLP is there is no property interest in the specific partnership assets. A limited partner is solely an investor. A limited partner may not actively participate in the conduct of the business. Assuming the limited partner complies with these conditions, s/he is not liable as a general partner on business debts and obligations, except to the extent of his capital contribution. "[T]he most striking feature of the relation of a special partner to the co-partnership is its detached and impersonal character which accentuates sharply its dissimilarity from the relations of a general partner." (Evans v. Galardi (1976) 16 Cal.3d 300, 309.) Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing to overcome defendant's relevancy and privacy objections based on the pleadings, discovery to date, discussions during mediation that are inadmissible for all purposes pursuant to Evid. Code 1119, or the prosecution of the conversion cause of action asserted solely against the general partnership. Monetary sanctions is granted in the total amount of $4,000.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JEFF NUSSBAUM, ESQ., 415.777.2220, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P.
GERALD MURPHY, ESQ., 415.356.4689, FOR CROSS DEFENDANTS LIBERTY ASSET MANAGEMENT CORP,. LUCY GAO, BENNY KO, TED HSU AND RED DOT INVESTMENTS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: CROSS DEFENDANTS LIBERTY ASSET MANAGEMENT CORP., LUCY GAO, BENNY KO, RED DOT INVESTMENTS, INC & TED HSU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendants Liberty Asset Management Corp., Lucy Gao, and Benny Ko's demurrer to Cross-Complainant UN Plaza Associates, L.P.'s Cross-Complaint is overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Cross-Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. The demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as Cross-Complainant has alleged sufficient facts, with particularity, to establish a claim for intentional misrepresentation. (Cross-Complaint, 13, 17-26.) Cross-Complainant alleges that Cross-Defendants, through their agent, misrepresented the existing facts that the potential purchasers were all cash buyers with a UBS account containing all of the cash to buy the property. (Cross Complaint, 13.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is overruled as Cross-Complainant has alleged sufficient facts, with particularity, that Cross-Defendants, through their agent, negligently misrepresented to Cross-Complainant that the potential purchaser was an all cash buyer that had no need for a pre-closing loan or additional equity. (Cross-Complaint, 28.) The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for alter ego is overruled as Cross-Complainant has alleged sufficient facts that a unity of interest existed between Cross-Defendants and that adherence to the separate existed would sanction fraud and promote injustice. (Cross-Complaint, 50-51.) The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant is given 10 days leave, if it can do in good faith, to plead additional facts that Cross-Defendants Hsu and Red Dot intentionally induced Cross-Complainants to rely on their misrepresentation(s). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
